title: Blyth's REN Architecture: A Prescription for IT Success
author: Aberdeen Group
source_file: 1996 Blyth's REN Architecture_ A Prescription for IT Success pr.pdf
abstract: Aberdeen Group evaluates Blyth Software's REN architecture (Real solutions, Endless possibilities, No boundaries), a dual-track application development framework targeting both traditional client-server and emerging Internet/Intranet commerce applications. The study argues that Blyth's OMNIS-based toolset, extended with five Net-enabled products (Web Enabler, OMNIS Data Server, Web Vault, Web Manager, Weblets), gives the company at least a 12-month competitive lead in mission-critical web development infrastructure. Aberdeen predicts that successful REN execution could elevate Blyth from a niche vendor to an IT household name.
importance_rationale: The study captured an important inflection point in 1996 web application development, anticipating enterprise-grade web middleware requirements; however, Blyth remained a niche player and did not achieve the breakthrough Aberdeen predicted.
relevance_rationale: The conceptual framework of 'Net development requiring client-server rigor foreshadowed today's enterprise web architecture principles; specific products are obsolete but the competitive analysis methodology transfers.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen predicted Blyth would become an 'IT household name' and carve out a long-term best-of-breed position in Net development; instead, Blyth/Omnis Technology never achieved mainstream status, was renamed multiple times (Omnis Technology -> Raining Data -> TigerLogic), and was ultimately sold to Cliftech Solutions in 2017.
